Title: Verify the app is available on both IOS and Android platforms.
Preconditions: User has access to a smartphone.
Test Steps:
1. Open the app store on the smartphone.
2. Search for the NeighborGood app.
3. Check if the app is available for download on both IOS and Android platforms.
Expected Result: The app is available for download on both IOS and Android platforms.

Title: Verify the scheduling of appointments and reservations for local businesses.
Preconditions: User has created an account and is logged in.
Test Data: A local business with available appointments.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the local business page.
2. Check if the available appointments are displayed.
3. Select an available appointment.
4. Confirm the appointment.
Expected Result: The appointment is successfully scheduled.

Title: Verify the donation or buying of goods at discounted prices.
Preconditions: User has created an account and is logged in.
Test Data: A discounted good available for donation or purchase.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the goods page.
2. Check if the discounted good is displayed.
3. Select the discounted good.
4. Choose to donate or purchase the good.
Expected Result: The good is successfully donated or purchased.

Title: Verify the sharing of tools with the community.
Preconditions: User has created an account and is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the tools page.
2. Check if the tool is displayed.
3. Select the tool for sharing.
4. Confirm the sharing of the tool.
Expected Result: The tool is successfully shared with the community.

Title: Verify the creation and management of buying groups to avoid intermediaries.
Preconditions: User has created an account and is logged in.
Test Data: A buying group to be created.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the buying groups page.
2. Check if the option to create a new buying group is available.
3. Fill in the necessary information to create the buying group.
4. Confirm the creation of the buying group.
Expected Result: The buying group is successfully created.

Title: Verify the strengthening of a sense of civic duty and belonging to the community.
Preconditions: User has created an account and is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the community page.
2. Check if there are opportunities for interaction with other community members.
3. Participate in a community interaction.
Expected Result: The user feels a stronger sense of civic duty and belonging to the community.

Title: Verify the provision of opportunities for vulnerable groups to interact with other community members and to address social issues.
Preconditions: User has created an account and is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the vulnerable group page.
2. Check if there are opportunities for interaction with other community members.
3. Participate in an interaction with a vulnerable group member.
Expected Result: The vulnerable group member feels more included in the community and social issues are addressed.

Title: Verify the functionality of the Flutter framework
Preconditions: The development environment is set up correctly.
Test Steps:
1. Open the project in the specified IDE (Android Studio or XCode)
2. Check if the Flutter framework is properly integrated
3. Build and run the application on an Android device
4. Build and run the application on an iOS device
Expected Result: The application should run successfully on both Android and iOS devices.

Title: Verify the real-time data update functionality
Preconditions: The application is running on both Android and iOS devices.
Test Data: Sample data to be updated in real-time.
Test Steps:
1. Start the application on both Android and iOS devices
2. Update the data on one device
3. Observe the data update on the other device
Expected Result: The data should update in real-time on both devices.

Title: Verify the geolocation functionality
Preconditions: The application is running on both Android and iOS devices.
Test Steps:
1. Start the application on both Android and iOS devices
2. Enable geolocation on both devices
3. Observe the geolocation data on both devices
Expected Result: The geolocation data should be accurate and updated on both devices.

Title: Verify the language accessibility
Preconditions: The application is running on both Android and iOS devices.
Test Steps:
1. Start the application on both Android and iOS devices
2. Change the language settings on both devices
3. Observe the language change on the application interface
Expected Result: The application should be accessible and understandable in multiple languages.

Title: Verify the search functionality
Preconditions: The application is running on both Android and iOS devices.
Test Steps:
1. Start the application on both Android and iOS devices
2. Use the search functionality on both devices
3. Observe the search results on both devices
Expected Result: The search functionality should work correctly on both devices.

Title: Verify the user role functionality
Preconditions: The application is running on both Android and iOS devices.
Test Data: Sample data for different user roles.
Test Steps:
1. Start the application on both Android and iOS devices
2. Log in as different user roles on both devices
3. Observe the interface and functionality differences for each user role on both devices
Expected Result: The application should provide different interfaces and functionality for different user roles.

Title: Team Responsibility and Skills
Preconditions: Team members are assigned and have access to the required development tools.
Test Steps:
1. Each team member is responsible for their assigned tasks.
2. Each team member is expected to meet their deadlines.
3. Each team member is expected to use their skills and put in effort to complete the project.
Expected Result: The project is completed on time and meets the project requirements.

Title: Required Skills
Preconditions: Team members are assigned and have access to the required development tools.
Test Steps:
1. Team members must have knowledge of the Dart programming language.
2. Team members must have knowledge of the Flutter framework.
3. Team members must have knowledge of NoSQL databases.
4. Team members must be familiar with the Android Studio, VSCode, XCode, and GitHub development environments.
5. Team members must have basic knowledge of Git and Bash git tools.
Expected Result: Team members have the required skills to complete the project.

Title: Risk Management
Preconditions: Team members are assigned and have access to the required development tools.
Test Steps:
1. Identify potential risks that could impact the project.
2. Assess the probability and impact of each risk.
3. Develop a plan to mitigate each identified risk.
Expected Result: The project is completed with minimal impact from identified risks.

Title: Communication within the Team
Preconditions: Team members are assigned and have access to the required development tools.
Test Steps:
1. Use WhatsApp for service communications.
2. Use Google Drive for document collaboration.
3. Use Git and GitHub for code versioning.
Expected Result: Effective communication within the team.

Title: Staffing Plan
Preconditions: Team members are assigned and have access to the required development tools.
Test Steps:
1. Identify the required skills for the project.
2. Assign team members based on their skills.
3. Ensure that team members have the necessary tools and resources to complete their tasks.
Expected Result: The project is completed with the required skills and resources.

Title: Development Environment
Preconditions: Team members are assigned and have access to the required development tools.
Test Steps:
1. Use Android Studio, VSCode, or XCode for development.
2. Use the Flutter and Dart programming languages for development.
3. Use Firebase for data storage.
Expected Result: The development environment is set up correctly for the project.

Title: Testing
Preconditions: Team members are assigned and have access to the required development tools.
Test Steps:
1. Test the application on Android devices with Android 6.0+ and the Google Play Services 11.0+.
2. Implement testing frequently during development.
3. Ensure that the code adheres to the standards of object-oriented programming and reusability, non-redundancy, modularity, and scalability.
Expected Result: The application is tested thoroughly and meets the required standards.

Title: Support Functions
Preconditions: Team members are assigned and have access to the required development tools.
Test Steps:
1. Implement a quality control process for the code.
2. Implement a configuration management process for the code.
Expected Result: The project is completed with effective quality control and configuration management.

Title: Verify the login functionality
Preconditions: The user has installed the application and opened it.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login screen.
2. Enter the valid user credentials.
3. Click on the login button.
Expected Result: The user is successfully logged in and redirected to the home screen.

Title: Verify the registration functionality
Preconditions: The user has installed the application and opened it.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the registration screen.
2. Enter the new user credentials.
3. Click on the register button.
Expected Result: The new user is successfully registered and redirected to the login screen.

Title: Verify the post creation functionality
Preconditions: The user is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the create post screen.
2. Enter the post content.
3. Click on the create post button.
Expected Result: The post is successfully created and displayed on the home screen.

Title: Verify the post deletion functionality
Preconditions: The user is logged in and has created a post.
Test Steps:

2. Click on the delete post button.
Expected Result: The post is successfully deleted and no longer visible on the home screen.

Title: Verify the comment functionality

Test Steps:

2. Enter the comment content.
3. Click on the post to add the comment.
Expected Result: The comment is successfully added to the post and displayed on the post details screen.

1. Navigate to the post details screen.
2. Click on the like button.
Expected Result: The post is successfully liked and the like count is incremented by 1.

2. Enter the search query.
3. Click on the search button.
Expected Result: The search results are displayed based on the search query.

Preconditions: The user is logged in.
